events.label: shell (exit 127)
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && cat > /tmp/answer.tmp <<'EOF'
{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"critical\",
  \"work_stage\": \"in-progress\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"non-compliant\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 20,
  \"value\": 0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.96,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [
    \"prepare-for-shutdown\",
    \"shut-down-equipment\",
    \"isolate-energy-sources\",
    \"apply-personal-lock-and-tag\",
    \"release-or-restrain-stored-energy\",
    \"verify-zero-energy\"
  ],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Breaker is only taped off; red tape is not a lockout device and does not physically prevent re-energization.\",
    \"Tag is taped to the panel door rather than secured at the energy-isolating device and does not provide lockout-equivalent protection.\",
    \"Checklist skips verification of zero energy before service work.\",
    \"Stored mechanical energy in the raised conveyor section is not identified, blocked, lowered, restrained, or otherwise controlled.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Stop servicing work and keep personnel clear of the conveyor until LOTO is corrected.\",
    \"Apply an authorized personal lockout device to the breaker or approved breaker lockout hasp, with an identifying tag attached to the lockout point.\",
    \"Update and follow the energy-control procedure to identify all electrical and mechanical energy sources for the conveyor.\",
    \"Lower, block, pin, crib, or otherwise restrain the raised conveyor section and dissipate or control all stored and residual energy.\",
    \"Verify isolation and zero energy by attempting start controls and using appropriate test methods before any service work continues.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"The LOTO sequence is not acceptable for in-progress service. Isolation has not been secured with an approved lockout device, the tag placement is inadequate, stored mechanical energy has not been controlled, and the required verification step is missing. Minimum correction is to restart the energy-control sequence from isolation, apply proper lock/tag hardware, control stored energy, and verify zero energy before continuing.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(c)(4)(ii)\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(c)(5)\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(4)\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(5)\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(6)\"
  ]
}
EOF
mv /tmp/answer.tmp /app/answer.json
jq . /app/answer.json"
→ /app
/bin/sh: 46: jq: not found
